skill.name: error-diagnostics-error-trace
skill.description: You are an error tracking and observability expert specializing in implementing comprehensive error monitoring solutions. Set up error tracking systems, configure alerts, implement structured logging, and ensure teams can quickly identify and resolve production issues.
skill.summary: Error tracking and observability expert skill for implementing comprehensive error monitoring solutions
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 33 static findings are false positives. The skill provides educational code samples for implementing standard error tracking (Sentry integration, structured logging, alerting). Network calls are legitimate error reporting to monitoring services. No malicious behavior detected.

content.user_title: Set Up Error Tracking and Monitoring
content.value_statement: Implement comprehensive error monitoring with Sentry integration, intelligent alerting, and real-time dashboards to quickly identify and resolve production issues.
actual_capabilities: Set up Sentry error tracking with custom integrations and middleware
Implement structured logging with Winston and Elasticsearch
Configure intelligent alert rules for error rate thresholds
Create error grouping and deduplication algorithms
Build real-time error monitoring dashboards with React
Implement automatic error recovery with circuit breakers
limitations: Does not provide live error tracking infrastructure
Requires existing error tracking service account for full functionality
Code samples need adaptation for specific project requirements
Does not include pre-configured production environments

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Setting Up Monitoring
use_cases.description: Implement error tracking across microservices with centralized logging and intelligent alerting

use_cases.title: Full-Stack Developer Adding Error Handling
use_cases.description: Integrate Sentry into Node.js or Python applications with custom error context

use_cases.title: Engineering Manager Improving Observability
use_cases.description: Set up real-time error dashboards and establish error response workflows

prompt_templates.title: Basic Error Tracking Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up error tracking for a Node.js Express application. I need to capture uncaught exceptions, unhandled promise rejections, and log them to Sentry with custom context.

prompt_templates.title: Structured Logging Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a structured logger using Winston that logs errors with stack traces, user context, and request details. Include Elasticsearch transport for production environments.

prompt_templates.title: Alert Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure intelligent alert rules for: high error rate (>5%), response time degradation (>1s), and memory usage critical (>90%). Set up Slack and PagerDuty channels with cooldown periods.

prompt_templates.title: Error Recovery System
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement an automatic error recovery system with circuit breaker pattern. Handle network errors with retry and backoff, database errors with read replica fallback, and rate limit errors with delay.

output_examples.input: Help me set up error tracking for a Node.js Express application
output: Sentry initialization with Express middleware configured
Global error handlers for uncaughtException and unhandledRejection
Request handler middleware for capturing request context
Custom error handler with status code filtering
User context enrichment for error tracking

output_examples.input: Create an alert system for high error rates
output: AlertRule configuration with threshold and time window
Slack webhook integration with formatted payloads
Cooldown mechanism to prevent alert spam

best_practices: Always include structured error context (user ID, request ID, environment)
Filter sensitive data like passwords, tokens, and API keys before sending to error trackers
Use error grouping to reduce noise from similar repeated errors
Set up appropriate sampling rates for high-traffic applications
anti_patterns: Do not log full request bodies containing sensitive user data
Avoid capturing errors without any context or user information
Do not set alert thresholds too low causing alert fatigue
Avoid ignoring or swallowing errors without proper logging

faq.question: What error tracking services does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill provides examples for Sentry and custom error tracking services. The code samples are adaptable to other services like Rollbar, Bugsnag, or custom solutions.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Sentry account?
faq.answer: The skill provides code templates that work with Sentry. You will need a Sentry account and DSN to fully configure the integration.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Python applications?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes Python code samples for alert management and error analysis that work with Python applications.

faq.question: How do I handle sensitive data in error logs?
faq.answer: Use the sanitization functions shown in the skill to filter sensitive keys like passwords, tokens, and API keys before sending errors.

faq.question: Does this skill work with frontend JavaScript errors?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on backend error tracking. Frontend error tracking typically requires the Sentry JavaScript SDK with different configuration.

faq.question: Can I create custom error grouping logic?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes error fingerprinting algorithms that can be customized to group errors based on your specific patterns and requirements.
